prose: The positive call rests on curated mechanistic convergence: rotenone is registered as a Parkinson's disease stressor by CTD DirectEvidence and anchors a registered neuro-AOP [E1]. This supports an evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis in which the molecular initiating event—inhibition at the Complex-I Q-site—is grounded in MitoCarta3.0 subunits, propagating to an adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2]. Brain-penetrance modeling makes CNS exposure structurally plausible, satisfying a necessary precondition rather than proving delivery [E5]. Human cohort data (PD OR ~2.5; Tanner 2011 FAME) are directionally concordant with, but not confirmatory of, this hypothesis [E4]. The 56 ToxCast assay hits illustrate the postulated chain and serve only as corroboration—they are anti-diagnostic and cannot by themselves discriminate a true stressor [E3]. Together these strands support the assay_mechanism_recovered tier without asserting causation; the curated mechanism remains the sole decisional basis here.

citations.detail: Curated convergence: CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence AND registered neuro-AOP stressor.
MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
56 mechanistic assay hits illustrate the chain (corroboration only — anti-diagnostic, not a discriminator).
